summary: Matcha mentioned in fragrance context shows sustained consumer interest, but article is not beverage-focused; monitor separately from food trends.
Oat milk remains the dominant non-dairy choice in major markets; ensure consistent supply and quality to meet established customer expectations.
Major boba chain expanding aggressively with direct competition strategy against coffee giants; consider boba offerings to capture this growing segment.
Viral dessert trend with limited beverage application; consider as food pairing opportunity rather than core menu addition.
Starbucks operational improvements gaining positive media attention; focus on coffee quality and customer experience remains core to their strategy.
Lighthearted meme content about beer drinking; minimal actionable insight for coffee shop menu planning.
Major reformulation by market leader after 25 years signals renewed consumer interest; consider reviewing your chai offering and potential menu updates.
Protein powder deals are common; consider vegan protein options as a beverage modifier for health-conscious customers, but demand remains steady rather than surging.
Zodiac-themed content generates mild engagement but doesn't indicate new demand; espresso remains a staple menu item.
Matcha used as design inspiration in fashion; no new beverage trend signal detected.
Starbucks launching 12 new February items including Valentine's drinks; monitor seasonal offerings and consider limited-time promotions to compete
